_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of principles and procedures for personnel recruitment, selection, training, compensation and benefits, labor relations and negotiation, and personnel information systems.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of talent acquisition, retention, and development strategies specific to data and AI roles, including market compensation dynamics, skill assessment methodologies, career path design, and the organizational conditions that attract and retain scarce technical talent.
comments: O*NET covers all HR. WIDAI LS-K-002 covers data/AI talent management specifically. Strong overlap in recruitment, selection, training, compensation for technical roles. Secondary: LS-K-013 (workforce planning), LS-S-002 (talent pipeline building).
_comment_atlas_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (nist band: roughly as similar as dissimilar). The STS score reflects meaningful semantic overlap between the broader O*NET element and the narrower WIDAI KSA. The superset relationship is well-supported — the shared concepts are substantial enough for the model to detect cross-text alignment.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8832 indicates moderate token-level semantic alignment. Precision exceeds Recall, indicating that the FDE's semantic content is more tightly aligned with the KSA than the reverse — the KSA contains concepts or specificity beyond what the FDE covers. The P > R pattern is atypical for a Superset relationship — it suggests the KSA may contain specificity that extends beyond the FDE's scope. This warrants attention during human review, though it may reflect domain-specific terminology in the KSA that the FDE covers conceptually without matching at the token level.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Neutral-dominant (77.39%) — the model finds the descriptions related but neither implying nor contradicting each other. Neutral is common for Superset pairs where the scope gap is large — the FDE covers broader territory than the KSA, so the NLI model sees topical relatedness without strict logical implication. The contradiction component (22.47%) may reflect scope asymmetry rather than semantic conflict.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.5463) is close to the STS cross-encoder (0.5537). The small gap (0.01) suggests the semantic relationship is largely capturable through independent encoding — the descriptions share enough surface-level semantic features that cross-attention adds only marginal signal.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Low lexical overlap (9.09% of tokens shared). Some shared vocabulary exists (likely common functional words or broadly-used domain terms) but the bulk of each description uses distinct terminology.
